title: PRD: Activation Invariant Test Requirement at SD Completion Gate
executive_summary: Adds a new LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL gate (`activation-invariant-gate.js`) that blocks SD completion when an SD ships a schema+UI+worker chain but lacks an end-to-end test asserting the chain works against real data. Closes the 26th writer-consumer asymmetry witness at SD-orchestration scale by extending the canonical TESTING sub-agent rule-set. Adds one column (`product_requirements_v2.activation_test_id`), one side table (`activation_catalog_expectations`), one audit CLI (`scripts/audit-activation-chain.mjs`), and a one-off retroactive scanner. Layer A (SD-FDBK-REFAC-LEO-CREATE-003-001 PR #3758) closed SD-creation fragmentation; this PRD closes Layer B (completion-gate has no cross-SD activation invariant).
business_context: Three GVOS sibling SDs shipped as 'completed' on 2026-05-13. Each individual SD met its narrow scope and passed all gates. But the integrated chain failed silently: gvos_archetypes catalog has 0 rows (seed never written), gvos_prompt_rubrics table does not exist (migration 007 absent), venture_gvos_profile has 0 rows across all 574 ventures (S11 worker never updated). Every venture's S17 view silently renders the legacy 4-variant Bootstrap pipeline, NOT the GVOS Awwwards-tier output the strategic intent specified. The completion gate had no mechanism to catch this. This SD installs that mechanism.
technical_context: Stack: JavaScript ESM. New dep: acorn (MIT, ~200KB) for FR-4 audit utility's call-graph analysis. Repository: EHG_Engineer monorepo. Gate registration is manual via getRequiredGates() in scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates.js lines 1150-1224. Sub-agent evidence rows write to sub_agent_execution_results with canonical insert shape from lib/sub-agent-executor/results-storage.js lines 267-327 (5-min dedupe window on sd_id+sub_agent_code). Bypass flag pattern from BaseExecutor.js: --bypass-validation --bypass-reason already exists; we tag activation-invariant skips inside the reason text (ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:<ticket>) rather than adding a new flag.

functional_requirements.title: Define activation-invariant test class
functional_requirements.description: Establish a new required-test class identified by product_requirements_v2.activation_test_id. Trigger: any SD whose key_changes (via DUAL-SCAN: structured type IN (schema,feature,ui,database) AND free-text regex over change|detail|title|impact|description) match the schema+UI+worker chain pattern. The test must operate against a real (or migration-applied test) database, execute the schema -> worker -> UI path end-to-end, and assert a non-trivial DOM/state outcome.
acceptance_criteria: New column product_requirements_v2.activation_test_id TEXT NULL exists in production DB
Column documented via COMMENT ON COLUMN with @activation tag convention
Reading PRDs: rows pre-existing this migration return NULL (read by CI guard as 'no chain, no test required')
CLAUDE_PLAN.md + CLAUDE_EXEC.md sections describe when to populate the field and what the test shape looks like (example reference + 1 sibling-SD diff)

functional_requirements.title: Completion-gate enforcement at LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL
functional_requirements.description: New gate activation-invariant-gate.js registered in gates.js::getRequiredGates() for LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL. For trigger-class SDs, gate PASSES if PRD.activation_test_id is non-empty AND the test file exists AND a TESTING sub-agent evidence row with verdict=PASS and metadata.activation_invariant_verified=true exists within 24h. Gate FAILS with remediation block (CLI tag [ACTIVATION_INVARIANT_AUDIT]) listing missing component, example diffs, and re-run command. Bypass via existing --bypass-validation --bypass-reason 'ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:<ticket>' (no new flag added).
acceptance_criteria: Gate registered and visible in handoff.js precheck LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL output
Gate signature matches { name, validator, required } and returns { passed, score, max_score, issues, warnings, details }
Trigger evaluator implements DUAL-SCAN (structured type match AND free-text regex on the heterogeneous key_changes shape)
Gate uses phase='LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL' exactly when querying sub_agent_execution_results
Bypass reason ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:<ticket> recognized; rate-limited to 3/SD via existing handoff bypass quota; logged to audit_log
Intentionally-incomplete test SD (synthetic fixture) is BLOCKED by the gate

functional_requirements.title: Catalog-empty CI guard
functional_requirements.description: New side table activation_catalog_expectations (sd_id uuid FK, table_name text, seed_migration_path text, created_at timestamptz) PK=(sd_id, table_name). For SDs that introduce catalog/registry tables expected to be seeded, PLAN authors INSERT rows declaring the expectation. CI workflow runs node scripts/check-activation-catalog.mjs <SD-ID> post-migration: for each declared expectation, asserts SELECT COUNT(*) > 0 FROM <table_name>. FAILS with link to the seed migration that was supposed to land.
acceptance_criteria: Side table created with FK to strategic_directives_v2(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
RLS policies match existing strategic_directives_v2 patterns (service-role write, authenticated read)
scripts/check-activation-catalog.mjs reads expectations and asserts non-zero counts
GitHub Actions workflow step added to existing CI matrix (does NOT create new workflow file)
Intentionally-unseeded catalog SD fixture is BLOCKED by the CI guard

functional_requirements.title: Activation-chain audit utility
functional_requirements.description: New CLI node scripts/audit-activation-chain.mjs <SD-ID>. Reads SD's metadata + PRD (schema migrations + UI components touched). For each touched table: COUNT(*) + report. For each touched UI testid: render synthetic venture + assert presence. Outputs activation-chain coverage score (0-5 dimensions: registry, route, menu, flag, E2E). Dual mode: default human table; --json for machine consumption. Exit code: 0 if >=4/5, 1 otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: Utility runs against SD-GVOS-COMPOSER-SNAPSHOTLOCKED-REGISTRY-ORCH-001 and outputs a coverage score <4/5 (empirical premise validation)
Utility runs against a known-good completed SD and outputs >=4/5
JSON output schema documented in script header comment
Test fixtures live in tests/fixtures/activation-chain/ (good + bad samples)

functional_requirements.title: Document the Activation Invariant Rule
functional_requirements.description: Add a new section to CLAUDE_PLAN.md (PRD authoring) and CLAUDE_EXEC.md (test writing) explaining when activation_test_id is required, what the test should look like, and link to FR-4 audit utility.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE_PLAN.md section under '## Activation Invariant Rule' added with: trigger conditions, PRD field reference, example
CLAUDE_EXEC.md section under '## Activation Invariant Test Pattern' added with: test layout, real-DB vs migration-applied-test-DB guidance, assertion patterns
Both files regenerated via node scripts/generate-claude-md-from-db.js if DB-sourced — verify via lint pass

functional_requirements.title: Retroactive scan of completed SDs
functional_requirements.description: One-off scripts/one-off/scan-completed-sds-for-activation-gap.mjs. Find SDs marked completed since 2026-01-01 that match FR-1 trigger. Run FR-4 audit. Report SDs with coverage_score < 0.9 as suspected gaps. Each gap files a feedback row with stable title [ACTIVATION_CHAIN_GAP] SD-<KEY> missing: <components> (idempotent via SELECT-then-INSERT dedupe per auto-resolve-recovered.js pattern). Remediation per finding is OUT OF SCOPE — separate SD per gap.
acceptance_criteria: Scanner runs to completion without error on full historical SD set
Re-running scanner does NOT create duplicate feedback rows (verified by row count before/after second run)
Feedback rows include JSON code-fence in body with coverage + missing list (machine-parseable)
Feedback rows tagged category='harness_backlog' for campaign routing
Final report includes summary: Scanned N completed SDs; M flagged with gap; P% coverage average

non_functional_requirements.title: Performance budget for activation invariant gate
non_functional_requirements.description: FR-2 activation-invariant gate adds <2 seconds to LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL handoff. Both the PRD query (single row by sd_id, indexed) and the sub_agent_execution_results query (single row by sd_id+sub_agent_code, 24h window, indexed) must complete within this budget. Measured via [GATE_LOG] structured timing.

non_functional_requirements.title: Retroactive scanner idempotency
non_functional_requirements.description: FR-6 scan-completed-sds-for-activation-gap.mjs must be safely re-runnable. Subsequent runs against the same historical SD set produce zero net new feedback rows. Dedupe key: (category=harness_backlog, title startsWith [ACTIVATION_CHAIN_GAP] SD-<KEY>, status NOT IN resolved|wont_fix).

non_functional_requirements.title: Backward compatibility with 3170 existing SDs
non_functional_requirements.description: New column product_requirements_v2.activation_test_id is NULL-safe and NULL-default. Existing SDs and PRDs without this field do NOT trigger the gate. Gate only fires when DUAL-SCAN heuristic matches the schema+UI+worker chain pattern (strict regex anchors).

non_functional_requirements.title: Observability via structured [GATE_LOG]
non_functional_requirements.description: All activation-invariant gate decisions emit a [GATE_LOG] JSON line including: gate_name, sd_id, verdict, score, trigger_matched (boolean + reason), evidence_row_id_if_found, elapsed_ms. Format matches existing GATE_LOG convention in gates.js.

non_functional_requirements.title: Bypass auditability via existing audit_log
non_functional_requirements.description: Bypass invocations via --bypass-validation --bypass-reason 'ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:<ticket>' write to the existing audit_log table with severity=warning, source=activation_invariant_gate, sd_id, reason_text, quota_remaining. No new audit channel created.

technical_requirements.title: ESM JavaScript on Node 20+
technical_requirements.description: All new files use ECMAScript modules (import/export). Compatible with the existing EHG_Engineer codebase Node 20+ runtime. No TypeScript compilation step added.

technical_requirements.title: Acorn parser for FR-4 audit utility
technical_requirements.description: Add acorn ^8.x as a runtime dependency (MIT-licensed, ~200KB, zero transitive deps). Used only by scripts/audit-activation-chain.mjs to parse JS imports/exports and build a call-graph identifying UI testids touched by the SD's modified components.

technical_requirements.title: Supabase service-role connection
technical_requirements.description: All DB access in new scripts uses scripts/lib/supabase-connection.js::createSupabaseServiceClient. This pooler-aware helper handles connection lifecycle correctly (avoids raw direct-URL timeouts; see CLAUDE_CORE.md Supabase Database Operations section).

technical_requirements.title: Manual gate registration in getRequiredGates()
technical_requirements.description: New activation-invariant-gate.js is pushed to the array returned by getRequiredGates() in scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates.js (lines 1150-1224). No auto-discovery; the explicit registration is intentional and matches all existing gates.

technical_requirements.title: Canonical sub-agent evidence row shape
technical_requirements.description: TESTING sub-agent evidence rows for activation-invariant verification follow the canonical lib/sub-agent-executor/results-storage.js insert shape: sd_id (uuid), sub_agent_code='TESTING', phase='LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL', verdict, confidence, metadata.activation_invariant_verified=true. 5-minute dedupe window on (sd_id, sub_agent_code) means re-runs UPDATE in place.

technical_requirements.title: Existing bypass flag with reason-text discriminator
technical_requirements.description: No new --bypass-* flag added. Bypass is via existing --bypass-validation --bypass-reason 'ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:<ticket>'. The gate's bypass evaluator regex-matches the reason text for the ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED prefix and applies the same per-SD/global quota (3/SD, 10/day) already enforced by handoff.js.

system_architecture:
## Failure Chain (Pipeline Ordering)

Layer 1 (CLOSED, PR #3758): leo-create-sd.js auto-router fragmented chairman locks
  -> Layer 2 (THIS SD): Each fragment's completion gate had no cross-SD activation invariant
    -> Layer 3 (downstream remediation, OUT OF SCOPE): GVOS-ACTIVATION-REMEDIATION-001 backfills specific gap

## Components (FR-ordered upstream-to-downstream)
FR-1 (column + convention) -> FR-2 (gate + sub-agent rule) -> FR-3 (CI catalog guard) -> FR-4 (audit utility) -> FR-5 (doc) -> FR-6 (retroactive scan, consumes FR-4)

## Component Inventory
- NEW Activation invariant gate: scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/activation-invariant-gate.js
- MODIFIED Gate registration in scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates.js (push to getRequiredGates)
- MODIFIED TESTING sub-agent rule extension at .claude/agents/testing-agent.md
- NEW Audit utility scripts/audit-activation-chain.mjs
- NEW Catalog CI guard scripts/check-activation-catalog.mjs
- NEW Retroactive scanner scripts/one-off/scan-completed-sds-for-activation-gap.mjs
- NEW Migration adding activation_test_id column to product_requirements_v2
- NEW Migration creating activation_catalog_expectations side table
- MODIFIED CLAUDE_PLAN.md + CLAUDE_EXEC.md doc sections (FR-5)
- NEW Trigger heuristic helper scripts/modules/activation-invariant/trigger-evaluator.js
- NEW Test fixtures tests/fixtures/activation-chain/{good,bad}/*.json
- NEW Vitest specs scripts/modules/activation-invariant/*.test.js
data_model:
Two schema additions:

1. product_requirements_v2.activation_test_id TEXT NULL — relative path to the activation invariant test spec (e.g., tests/e2e/activation-invariant/gvos-chain.spec.ts). NULL means no chain in this PRD.
   Migration: ALTER TABLE product_requirements_v2 ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS activation_test_id TEXT;
   Annotation: COMMENT ON COLUMN product_requirements_v2.activation_test_id IS '@activation reference to required test for invariant verification'

2. activation_catalog_expectations side table (PK on sd_id+table_name, FK to strategic_directives_v2(id) ON DELETE CASCADE):
   Columns: sd_id uuid, table_name text, seed_migration_path text, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), created_by text
   Index on table_name for catalog-wide queries
   RLS: service_role write, authenticated read (matches strategic_directives_v2 patterns)

Both migrations NULL-safe; backward-compatible across 3170 existing SDs (verified by DATABASE sub-agent).

test_scenarios.title: Gate blocks intentionally-incomplete activation chain
test_scenarios.scenario: Given a synthetic fixture SD with key_changes containing schema+ui types AND PRD.activation_test_id=NULL, when LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL precheck runs, then GATE_ACTIVATION_INVARIANT FAILS with score<70 and remediation message naming the missing component.

test_scenarios.title: Gate passes when activation_test_id present and PASS evidence exists
test_scenarios.scenario: Given fixture SD with key_changes containing schema+ui AND PRD.activation_test_id set AND a TESTING sub_agent_execution_results row with verdict=PASS and metadata.activation_invariant_verified=true within 24h, when handoff runs, gate PASSES with score 100.

test_scenarios.title: Trigger heuristic handles heterogeneous key_changes
test_scenarios.scenario: Given SD-GVOS-S17-PROMPT-QUALITY-ORCH-001 actual key_changes (which use {title, detail} not {type}), when trigger evaluator runs, it matches via DUAL-SCAN free-text regex over change|detail|title|impact|description.

test_scenarios.title: Catalog CI guard fails on unseeded registry
test_scenarios.scenario: Given a fixture SD with an activation_catalog_expectations row pointing at an empty test table, when check-activation-catalog.mjs runs, it exits 1 and prints the seed migration path that was supposed to land.

test_scenarios.title: Audit utility correctly scores GVOS empirical premise
test_scenarios.scenario: Given SD-GVOS-COMPOSER-SNAPSHOTLOCKED-REGISTRY-ORCH-001 (real production data), when audit-activation-chain.mjs runs, output coverage <4/5 with non-empty missing list. Validates empirical premise.

test_scenarios.title: Retroactive scanner idempotency
test_scenarios.scenario: Run scan-completed-sds-for-activation-gap.mjs twice in succession. Second run produces same set of feedback rows; no duplicate INSERTs. Verified by SELECT count comparison.

test_scenarios.title: Bypass discriminator works via existing reason flag
test_scenarios.scenario: Given a trigger-class SD without activation_test_id, when handoff executes with --bypass-validation --bypass-reason ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:JIRA-123, gate logs bypass to audit_log, decrements quota counter, and proceeds. Quota exhaustion (4th bypass on same SD) blocks.

acceptance_criteria: All 7 test scenarios pass
GATE_ACTIVATION_INVARIANT visible in handoff.js precheck LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL output
TESTING sub-agent evidence row schema documented in .claude/agents/testing-agent.md
FR-4 audit utility produces score<4/5 for SD-GVOS-COMPOSER-SNAPSHOTLOCKED-REGISTRY-ORCH-001 (empirical premise validation)
FR-6 scanner runs to completion against full history; emits >=1 feedback row; re-run produces 0 net new rows
Both migrations apply cleanly via database-agent (no SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD required when SUPABASE_POOLER_URL set)
Memory entry filed: 26th writer-consumer asymmetry witness PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001
Russian Judge PRD score >= 70
Total PR set: <=5 PRs, each <=100 LOC ideal, <=200 LOC max with justification

risks.risk: Trigger heuristic false-positive rate too high (every UI-tweak SD triggers the gate)
risks.mitigation: DUAL-SCAN with strict regex anchors; require BOTH schema-type AND (worker|consumer) phrase match. Test against 50 recent completed SDs as part of FR-4; tune until false-positive < 10%. Document tuning constants at top of trigger-evaluator.js.

risks.risk: Process friction: another required test per qualifying SD adds EXEC overhead
risks.mitigation: Bounded scope: one test per affected surface, NOT one per FR. Use bypass discriminator for genuinely-deferred chains with ticket reference. Quota = 3/SD enforces accountability.

risks.risk: Schema migration applied before code that reads it (FR-1 column before FR-2 gate)
risks.mitigation: PR ordering: migration PR ships FIRST and is independently revertable. Gate code reads coalesce(activation_test_id, NULL) so absence of column would NOT crash (NULL default).

risks.risk: Retroactive scanner overwhelms feedback inbox with 50+ rows on first run
risks.mitigation: First run gated behind --commit flag (dry-run by default). LEAD reviews dry-run output before committing. Rows tagged category=harness_backlog (not critical) so triage routing handles them.

risks.risk: Activation test takes too long to run in CI (real DB + UI render)
risks.mitigation: Use migration-applied test database with seed fixtures (NOT production). Render via vitest+jsdom or playwright-component. Target <30s per activation test.

constraints: Must NOT create a new sub-agent code — extend TESTING (per DATABASE sub-agent decision; avoids gate-pipeline rewiring at every handoff)
Must NOT add a new --bypass-* flag — discriminate via --bypass-reason text (ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED:<ticket>)
Migration must be NULL-safe and backward-compatible (3170 existing SDs)
Stay within 5 PRs total; each <=100 LOC ideal
assumptions: Supabase service-role client is available in handoff.js context (already true)
PRDs created via add-prd-to-database.js consistently populate activation_test_id when PLAN author follows updated CLAUDE_PLAN.md (FR-5 enforces this via documentation)
The 3 GVOS sibling SDs (now completed=true) are NOT remediated by this SD — remediation is the separate SD-GVOS-ACTIVATION-REMEDIATION-001
TESTING sub-agent already runs at LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL handoff (confirmed via gates.js inspection)
goal_summary: Add a LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL gate that requires SDs shipping schema+UI+worker chains to have a real-DB end-to-end test asserting the chain works. Closes 26th writer-consumer asymmetry witness at SD-orchestration scale.
implementation_approach: Five-PR sequence ordered upstream-to-downstream (matches Failure Chain in system_architecture). PR1: Migrations (activation_test_id column + activation_catalog_expectations side table). PR2: trigger-evaluator.js helper + gate (activation-invariant-gate.js) + register in getRequiredGates(). PR3: TESTING sub-agent rule extension (.claude/agents/testing-agent.md) + bypass quota wiring. PR4: audit-activation-chain.mjs (FR-4) + check-activation-catalog.mjs (FR-3). PR5: CLAUDE_PLAN.md + CLAUDE_EXEC.md doc sections (FR-5) + retroactive scanner (FR-6, dry-run by default). Each PR ≤100 LOC ideal, ≤200 LOC max with justification. Vitest specs ride with code in same PR. PR1 must merge before PR2-5 (migration dependency). PR3-5 can ship in any order after PR2.

consumers.journey: When an EXEC/PLAN session attempts to mark a trigger-class SD complete, the gate runs synchronously, blocks with remediation, and exits non-zero.
On gate failure, reads remediation message in console, writes the activation invariant test spec, populates PRD.activation_test_id via add-prd-to-database.js, re-runs handoff.

consumers.consumer: LEAD reviewer of FR-6 scanner output
consumers.journey: Receives summary report + feedback rows in /leo inbox; decides which historical gaps to remediate via follow-up SDs.
Post-migration workflow step runs check-activation-catalog.mjs; failure blocks PR merge.

dependencies.name: strategic_directives_v2.id (FK target)
dependencies.failure_mode: FK violation if SD deleted while expectation row exists (CASCADE handles)

dependencies.name: product_requirements_v2.sd_id (existing)
dependencies.failure_mode: Gate skips if no PRD row exists (logs warning, score 0/100)

dependencies.name: sub_agent_execution_results (TESTING evidence)
dependencies.failure_mode: Gate FAILS if no TESTING row within 24h with activation_invariant_verified=true; remediation: run testing-agent

dependencies.name: feedback table (FR-6 writes here)
dependencies.failure_mode: Race condition with auto-resolve-recovered.js: 5-min safety window mirrors orphan-qf-reaper.mjs pattern

dependencies.name: audit_log (bypass writes)
dependencies.failure_mode: audit_log unavailable -> bypass still proceeds but logs error (consistent with existing handoff.js bypass behavior)

integration_operationalization.data_contracts: Two new schema objects detailed in data_model field. Both NULL-safe, backward compatible, RLS-policied. No breaking changes to existing tables/columns. Sub-agent evidence rows extend metadata schema with metadata.activation_invariant_verified: boolean field (additive, optional).
integration_operationalization.runtime_config: No new env vars required for production. CI workflow may set ACTIVATION_INVARIANT_BYPASS_QUOTA_MULTIPLIER env (default 1.0) for test environments to relax quota during fixture testing. No feature flag needed — gate is on for all trigger-class SDs immediately. Deployment sequence: (1) migrations apply, (2) gate registered in next handoff.js release, (3) FR-6 scanner runs once in dry-run, then committed.
integration_operationalization.observability_rollout: Metrics: count of [GATE_LOG] GATE_ACTIVATION_INVARIANT lines in handoff logs (PASS vs FAIL), count of ACTIV-CHAIN-DEFERRED bypasses in audit_log. Rollout: gate is non-disruptive (FAIL on existing in-flight SDs is rare because trigger heuristic is strict). Rollback: revert PR for gates.js registration removes gate from chain; data migrations are additive (no rollback needed for NULL columns and unreferenced side table).

exploration_summary: Explore agent + DATABASE sub-agent inspected (1) scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/smoke-test-gate.js (signature, return shape, PRD access), (2) scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.js (bypass precedent), (3) scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates.js lines 1150-1224 (manual gate registration via getRequiredGates), (4) lib/sub-agent-executor/results-storage.js lines 267-327 (evidence insert shape, 5-min dedupe), (5) scripts/modules/inbox/auto-resolve-recovered.js lines 77-104 (idempotent feedback writer pattern), (6) database/migrations/010_add_prd_column_descriptions.sql (COMMENT ON TABLE precedent — plain English only, @activation tag is new convention), (7) .claude/agents/testing-agent.partial (sub-agent registration format). Conclusion: greenfield activation-invariant infrastructure; no duplicate gates/sub-agents/utilities exist; TESTING extension is canonical path (not new sub-agent code).
smoke_test_cmd: node scripts/handoff.js precheck LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL SD-LEO-INFRA-REQUIRE-END-END-001 && node scripts/audit-activation-chain.mjs SD-GVOS-COMPOSER-SNAPSHOTLOCKED-REGISTRY-ORCH-001

metadata.baseline_observation:
## Baseline Observation

Command run (2026-05-13 by SD-LEO-INFRA-REQUIRE-END-END-001 LEAD phase): empirical verification of GVOS sibling SD completion state.

Output observed: 3 GVOS sibling SDs all status=completed phase=COMPLETED; Layer A shipped via SD-FDBK-REFAC-LEO-CREATE-003-001 PR #3758 merged 2026-05-13; no duplicate gate exists (smoke-test-gate.js / phantom-test-audit-gate.js / wire-check-gate.js are orthogonal); no existing audit utility scripts/audit-activation-chain.mjs.

First point of failure (root cause): The completion gate for LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL has NO mechanism to assert that an SD shipping a schema+UI+worker chain has an end-to-end test verifying the chain works against real data. Each individual SD's narrow scope test passed in isolation; the cross-SD activation invariant was untested. Result: 3 SDs marked complete with feature-dark output to 574 ventures.

Expected output (after this SD ships): For any trigger-class SD without activation_test_id populated AND verified, LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL gate emits [ACTIVATION_INVARIANT_AUDIT] FAIL with remediation and exits non-zero. SD cannot be marked completed.

prd_bound_risks_addressed: expects_seed location locked to side table (FR-3)
